title: festuca elatior subsp. arundinacea
intro: Festuca elatior subsp. arundinacea, also known as tall fescue, is a subspecies of grass that belongs to the family Poaceae. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, making it effective in treating wounds and skin conditions. The health benefits of Festuca elatior subsp. arundinacea include reducing inflammation, improving digestion, and supporting immune function. The plant can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties and health benefits.
